scenarios.name: Full Angular 19 Migration + Standalone Conversion
scenarios.description: Complete migration from Angular 16 to 19, converting all 22 legacy NgModules to standalone components, eliminating zone.js in favour of Signals, upgrading all CDK/Material dependencies, and addressing all circular dependencies.
riskFactors: 3-major-version jump (16→17→18→19) with breaking changes at each step
4 circular dependency cycles to untangle before migration
Test coverage at 62% means regressions may go undetected
assumptions: Team of 7 frontend engineers available (DEV_01 through DEV_07)
No concurrent major feature work during migration sprints
Backend APIs remain stable during migration
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Fully modernised Angular 19 codebase with standalone components, Signals-based state, zero legacy NgModules, all circular dependencies resolved, and zone.js removed.

scenarios.name: Interim Angular 17 Upgrade + Critical Fixes Only
scenarios.description: Upgrade from Angular 16 to 17 (single major version), convert only the auth-core and payments modules to standalone (the two most problematic areas), fix the high-severity circular dependencies, and defer the full standalone/Signals migration to a later quarter.
riskFactors: Single major version jump (16→17) — well-documented upgrade path
Deferred tech debt: 20 of 22 NgModules remain legacy
zone.js remains, limiting future performance gains
Angular 17 LTS will expire in ~18 months, requiring another upgrade
assumptions: Core team of 4 senior engineers (DEV_01, DEV_02, DEV_03, DEV_07) with remaining team on feature work
Auth-core and payments modules are the highest-value standalone targets based on ticket overrun data
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Angular 17 codebase with auth-core and payments modules on standalone, high-severity circular deps resolved, remaining migration deferred to next quarter.
